original_prompt:
PORTFOLIO_CHILD_PROJECT: true
Parent portfolio: batch-cat-b2
Selected app slug: looseends

Build this app as a working local-first SwiftUI MVP.

## Selected App Spec

# Loose Ends

Build mode: **MVP build**.

## App Name

Loose Ends

## Category

Productivity

## One-Sentence Promise

Weekly ritual resurfaces one dormant personal project at a time to act on, snooze, or release

## Claude Design Handoff Prompt

(not supplied yet)

## Parent Portfolio Prompt

Build 6 completely separate production-ready iOS apps at the same time: exactly 3 Photo & Video apps and 3 Productivity apps.

This is a multi-app PORTFOLIO request: multiple apps in one program. Each selected app becomes its own separate project — one folder per app.


Requirements for every app:
1. Must go from 0 to production-ready, not just a few features.
2. Must be unique, useful, and commercially viable.
3. Must be beautifully designed and feel premium.
4. Must provide real end-user value.
5. Must have a realistic path to monetization — a subscription or paid offering with genuine value (never cosmetics-only).
6. Should have viral potential (broad or niche) without sacrificing usefulness.
7. Must be better than its competitors in a meaningful way.
8. Local-first where applicable, architected so cloud support can be added later without rewriting.

UNIQUENESS (hard rule, non-negotiable):
- No app in this batch may be similar to another app in this batch.
- No app may be similar to anything already built or being built in this workspace or before, including: Waylay (location-based personal recall utility), TrueScale, Provenance, Scope-Guard, Practice-Loop, Proof, ReturnWise, VerveCoach, CueKeeper, Countertop, FieldQuote, my existing digital temple app, and my existing PO-automation tool.
- No app may be similar to apps in the OTHER batches of this program (check sibling project folders in the workspace for their concepts).
- Do not reuse prior concepts, themes, or mechanics from earlier apps — even if the request sounds similar to something built before, produce a differentiated concept.

Bonus points (optional — only when integral and genuinely valuable, never forced): unique use of LLMs, AR, or ML.

Build rules:
- Run the app efforts in parallel; keep discussion/design phases separate per app.
- Each app gets its own full product direction, design direction, architecture, implementation plan, and build output.
- Save all phase discussions and final decisions; at the end combine each app's full transcript into a .txt file.
- If an app is liked, also prepare Jira board + Notion project structures for backfilling later.
- Use enough rounds per phase for high-quality discussion, ending early on consensus.
- Recover cleanly from stalls; report only important milestones.

Output:
- One folder per app in the output directory — never one folder containing all apps.
Output directory:
- /Users/pchordia/Documents/iOS-App-Factory

Now generate at least 12 candidate ideas, discuss them thoroughly, choose the best 6, and build all 6 to production quality.


## Change requested
The build produced sources but NO buildable Xcode project. Generate a complete working project wiring in all existing sources, then make it compile cleanly for the iOS Simulator.

phase_discussions.title: Scope the change
phase_discussions.output: You've hit your monthly spend limit · raise it at claude.ai/settings/usage

tasks.title: Project skeleton: Xcode target, bundle id, empty Info.plist, locked folder structure, libsqlite3 linked, green xcodebuild build
acceptance_criteria: xcodebuild build succeeds with zero source files beyond a placeholder root view
Info.plist has no usage-description keys
Domain/Persistence/Store/Entitlements/DesignSystem/Features folders exist and are correctly target-membership-included
folder layout matches the locked architecture doc exactly
libsqlite3 is linked and a trivial sqlite3_libversion() call compiles and runs, so T-003 never discovers a linker problem after the fact

tasks.title: Domain types + RitualEngine (pure, dependency-free)
acceptance_criteria: RitualEngine never calls Date() directly, takes now: Date everywhere

unit tests cover 14-day dormancy boundary, 7-day pacing, cold-start exception, tiebreak chain (oldest lastTouchedAt -> oldest createdAt -> UUID), negative-duration clock anomalies always resolving not-eligible, edit never resets lastTouchedAt or status
act/snooze/release each throw a typed error if projectID does not match snapshot.metadata.surfacedProjectID, with a named test proving this
ProjectStatus has exactly two cases, active and released; no other status is ever stored
no import of SwiftUI or UIKit anywhere in this folder, including in ViewData.swift, since that's what justifies these types living here instead of in primary_ui's folder

tasks.title: SQLite persistence: libsqlite3 wrapper + SQLiteProjectRepository
acceptance_criteria: WAL journal mode with synchronous = FULL
every mutation (act/snooze/release/delete-of-surfaced) is one atomic transaction covering projects, decisions, and metadata tables
corruption tests cover exactly two scenarios: surfacedProjectID pointing at a deleted project, and surfacedProjectID pointing at a .released project — no test asserts nil-surfaced is corruption
a perf test asserts a full mutation transaction against a database pre-loaded with several hundred history rows completes under 10ms
zero third-party SQL wrapper libraries; libsqlite3 only

tasks.title: ClockProviding + EntitlementProviding boundaries
acceptance_criteria: SystemClock is the only production ClockProviding implementation and is the sole source of now for AppStore and the repository
FreeTierEntitlements.maxFreeActiveProjects = 7 exists as an unenforced constant only, read nowhere inside RitualEngine.createProject

tasks.title: AppStore: synchronous glue between RitualEngine and ProjectRepository
acceptance_criteria: every AppStore method is fully synchronous, no Task{} wrapping anywhere in the call chain
full bootstrap -> mutate -> simulated-relaunch round trip test passes, including which project is surfaced
didRecoverFromCorruptionOnLastLoad is exposed as plain state, separate from AppError
a single named regression test proves creating an 8th, 9th, and 10th project through AppStore.createProject all still succeed and are never blocked by FreeTierEntitlements
AppStore is composed exactly once, in LooseEndsApp.swift, and that file is the only place other lanes may request changes to rather than edit directly

tasks.title: Design tokens + atomic components (ThreadGlyph, DateChip)
acceptance_criteria: ThreadGlyph renders all four ThreadStatus cases as bounded 28x28pt Path shapes (or the named SF Symbols fallback) with no per-project variation or particle effects
ThreadGlyph.accessibilityLabel and its visual tier are both derived from the same daysUntouched/date associated value
color tokens include no status-meaning color, only accentInteractive/Pressed/Subtle for tappable affordances
Dynamic Type scaling verified on DateChip at largest accessibility size

tasks.title: Composite components: SurfacedCard, RitualEmptyState, ProjectRecordForm, ProjectRow, HistoryRow, SecondaryNavMenu
acceptance_criteria: SurfacedCard renders all five co-equal sections (what it is / why it mattered / why it stalled / smallest next step / why it surfaced now) with no truncation and no internal scroll region
not marked complete until integrated against real AppStore output, not mock fixtures
Release is never color-coded differently from Act/Snooze as a failure state

tasks.title: Home screen (six states)
acceptance_criteria: all six HomeState cases render distinctly: loading, surfaced, noProjectsYet, noneEligibleYet(reason, date), allReleased, storeFailure
each state expresses its one-sentence plain-language explanation
never shows more than one surfaced project

tasks.title: Project Record screen (create + edit modes)
acceptance_criteria: future-dated lastTouchedAt shows a visible validation error, not silent clamping, at entry time
edit mode supports direct snooze-until correction with no separate undo control
delete confirmation copy forks explicitly when the target is the currently-surfaced project

tasks.title: Resolution sheets: Act, Snooze, Release
acceptance_criteria: Snooze shows only the three fixed intervals plus current snooze count and exact resulting next-eligible date before confirming
swipe-to-dismiss or tap-away on any sheet is a silent cancel that writes nothing
sheets never auto-advance to another surfaced project

tasks.title: Your Projects + History screens
acceptance_criteria: Your Projects has no filter/sort/badge affordance implying pick-what-surfaces-next
History renders correctly at zero, one, and hundreds of entries with no pagination bugs and fully static rows
History glyphs are restricted to taut/coiled/knotted only

tasks.title: Onboarding + Settings screens
acceptance_criteria: onboarding is a single screen, three plain sentences, zero permission prompts, ends by opening Project Record in create mode
Settings contains exactly one non-blocking premium-intent line with no tappable affordance and no dead end

tasks.title: Resilience utilities: BackupExclusionChecker, RedactedLogger, ThreadGlyphAccessibilityLabel
acceptance_criteria: automated test asserts the live SQLite file's isExcludedFromBackup is always false
RedactedLogger's function signature makes it impossible to pass user free-text content, only static event names and opaque UUIDs
ThreadGlyphAccessibilityLabel and ThreadGlyph consume the exact same ThreadStatus value so they cannot drift

tasks.title: Accessibility audit + narrow XCUITest flows
acceptance_criteria: XCUIApplication().performAccessibilityAudit() passes against the Home surfaced-card state at a large accessibility content size
full resolve-and-reappear cycle covered for each of Act/Snooze/Release
delete-the-surfaced-project warning copy path is covered
44pt minimum tap targets verified at largest Dynamic Type

tasks.title: Final integration checkpoint: full app smoke, App icon, launch screen, build verification
acceptance_criteria: xcodebuild build and full test suite green on a clean checkout
manual pass confirms zero network calls fire anywhere in the core loop
force-quit/relaunch preserves exact prior state including which project is surfaced
app does not ship until this checklist and the four-lane acceptance criteria above all agree on what works

tasks.title: Continuous privacy/compile gate: no-network-call scan, run against every lane's PR from day one
acceptance_criteria: scans the entire LooseEnds/ tree for URLSession, Network, or CFNetwork symbols and fails if any are found outside an explicitly allow-listed file
runs on every PR from every lane starting immediately after T-001 merges, not held until T-015
a failing scan blocks merge for whichever lane's PR triggered it, without polish_resilience needing write access to that lane's files

interfaces.name: ProjectID
interfaces.signature: struct ProjectID: Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let rawValue: UUID }
interfaces.notes: CONTESTED: Codex proposes wrapped ID structs; Claude proposes raw UUID directly on Project.id. Must pick one in round 2 — affects every cross-lane signature.

interfaces.name: DecisionID
interfaces.signature: struct DecisionID: Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let rawValue: UUID }
interfaces.notes: Immutable identity for append-only history rows.

interfaces.name: ProjectStatus
interfaces.signature: enum ProjectStatus: String, Codable, Sendable { case active, released }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED by convergence: both sides independently cut this to two cases. No .surfaced (lives only in metadata.surfacedProjectID), no .dormant or .snoozed (both are passive, time-derived facts, not events — storing them would require banned background recomputation).

interfaces.name: DecisionKind
interfaces.signature: enum DecisionKind: String, Codable, Sendable { case acted, snoozed, released }
interfaces.notes: Both sides agree delete never produces a decision row. Naming differs (Codex: DecisionKind/acted, Claude: DecisionType/act) — trivial to reconcile.

interfaces.name: SnoozeIntervalOption
interfaces.signature: enum SnoozeIntervalOption: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case twoWeeks, oneMonth, threeMonths }
interfaces.notes: Agreed by both sides; only naming differs (Codex: SnoozeIntervalOption, Claude: SnoozeInterval) — trivial, not a real conflict.

interfaces.name: ProjectRecord
interfaces.signature: struct ProjectRecord: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable { let id: ProjectID; let createdAt: Date; let updatedAt: Date; var title: String; var summary: String; var whyItMattered: String; var whyItStalled: String; var smallestNextStep: String?; var lastTouchedAt: Date; var snoozedUntil: Date?; var snoozeCount: Int; var releasedAt: Date?; var releaseNote: String?; var status: ProjectStatus }
interfaces.notes: Canonical persisted project model.

interfaces.name: DecisionRecord
interfaces.signature: struct DecisionRecord: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable { let id: DecisionID; let projectID: ProjectID; let projectTitleSnapshot: String; let occurredAt: Date; let kind: DecisionKind; let note: String?; let snoozeUntil: Date? }
interfaces.notes: Append-only ledger row with denormalized title for stable history rendering.

interfaces.name: AppMetadataRecord
interfaces.signature: struct AppMetadataRecord: Sendable, Codable { var schemaVersion: Int; var surfacedProjectID: UUID?; var lastResolvedAt: Date?; var hasCompletedOnboarding: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Single-row metadata model. Surfaced slot and pacing timestamp are persisted explicitly here, never query-derived.

interfaces.name: RitualSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct RitualSnapshot: Sendable { var projects: [Project]; var decisions: [DecisionLogEntry]; var metadata: AppMetadataRecord }
interfaces.notes: Both sides have an equivalent full-state snapshot struct (Codex: RitualSnapshot, Claude: StoreSnapshot). Naming needs reconciling but shape agrees.

interfaces.name: HomeScreenState
interfaces.signature: enum HomeScreenState: Sendable { case loading; case surfaced(SurfacedProjectViewData); case noProjectsYet; case noneEligibleYet(reason: String, nextEligibleAt: Date?); case allReleased; case storeFailure(AppError) }
interfaces.notes: CONTESTED: Codex's version carries a fully pre-assembled view-data struct (days-untouched, glyph state, why-surfaced-now already computed); Claude's version carries the raw Project and leaves derivation to callers. Must resolve — affects whether views ever compute display data themselves.

interfaces.name: SurfacedProjectViewData
interfaces.signature: struct SurfacedProjectViewData: Sendable { let projectID: UUID; let title: String; let whatItIs: String; let whyItMattered: String; let whyItStalled: String; let smallestNextStep: String?; let whySurfacedNow: String; let daysUntouched: Int; let snoozeCount: Int; let glyphState: ThreadStatus }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED: HomeState carries this pre-assembled struct, not a raw Project. Field names match Project's own field names exactly (whatItIs, not summary). Maps 1:1 onto the five co-equal labeled sections locked by the design phase.

interfaces.name: PlainMessageState
interfaces.signature: struct PlainMessageState: Sendable { let title: String; let message: String; let actionTitle: String? }
interfaces.notes: Reusable empty/waiting/error state payload.

interfaces.name: HistoryEntryViewData
interfaces.signature: struct HistoryEntryViewData: Identifiable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let projectID: UUID; let title: String; let occurredAt: Date; let type: DecisionType; let note: String?; let glyphState: ThreadStatus }
interfaces.notes: glyphState only ever .taut/.coiled/.knotted — frayed represents an undecided state that by definition is never logged.

interfaces.name: ProjectListItemViewData
interfaces.signature: struct ProjectListItemViewData: Identifiable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let title: String; let statusLine: String; let glyphState: ThreadStatus; let isSurfaced: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Your Projects row model; statusLine is a plain-language sentence assembled by RitualEngine/AppStore, never composed by the view.

interfaces.name: ProjectDraft
interfaces.signature: struct ProjectDraft: Sendable { var title: String; var whatItIs: String; var whyItMattered: String; var whyItStalled: String; var smallestNextStep: String; var lastTouchedAt: Date; func validate(now: Date) -> Result<Project, [AppError]> }
interfaces.notes: Transient create/edit form state, separate from the persisted Project. validate() calls RitualEngine.validateLastTouchedDate plus required-field checks.

interfaces.name: ThreadGlyphState
interfaces.signature: enum ThreadGlyphState: Sendable { case frayed(daysUntouched: Int); case taut; case coiled(until: Date); case knotted }
interfaces.notes: Both sides converge: History rows can only ever resolve to taut/coiled/knotted; frayed only ever appears on the live Home card. Naming differs (ThreadGlyphState vs ThreadStatus) but substance agrees.

interfaces.name: AppError
interfaces.signature: enum AppError: Error, Sendable { case validation(field: String, reason: String); case persistenceUnavailable; case saveFailed; case unrecoverableCorruption; case notFound; case unknown(message: String) }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED: deliberately has no 'recoveredCorruption' case — a successful silent self-heal is not a user-facing failure and shouldn't risk defensive banner UI. That fact is tracked separately via AppStore.didRecoverFromCorruptionOnLastLoad so QA can assert it without conflating self-heal with genuine, unrecoverable failure.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine
interfaces.signature: enum RitualEngine { static func homeState(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) -> HomeState; static func eligibleProjects(in projects: [Project], now: Date) -> [Project]; static func selectSurfaced(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) -> UUID?; static func createProject(from draft: ProjectDraft, snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan; static func updateProject(id: UUID, draft: ProjectDraft, snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan; static func resolveAct(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, projectID: UUID, nextStep: String, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan; static func resolveSnooze(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, projectID: UUID, interval: SnoozeInterval, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan; static func resolveRelease(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, projectID: UUID, closureNote: String?, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan; static func deleteProject(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, projectID: UUID, now: Date) -> RepositoryMutationPlan; static func recoverSurfacedState(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) -> RepositoryMutationPlan?; static func threadStatus(for project: Project, now: Date) -> ThreadStatus; static func threadStatus(for decision: DecisionType) -> ThreadStatus; static func validateLastTouchedDate(_ input: Date, now: Date) -> Date }
interfaces.notes: Deliberately a static pure namespace, not a protocol. No hidden state, no Date() calls inside, easy direct unit testing.

interfaces.name: RitualComputation
interfaces.signature: struct RitualComputation: Sendable { let repairedSnapshot: StoreSnapshot; let homeState: HomeState; let history: [HistoryEntryViewData]; let projects: [ProjectListItemViewData]; let recoveredFromCorruption: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Bootstrap output combining corruption-repair with derived view models in one pure call, so AppStore.bootstrap() has exactly one thing to call and publish.

interfaces.name: RepositoryMutationPlan
interfaces.signature: struct RepositoryMutationPlan: Sendable { let projectsToUpsert: [Project]; let projectIDsToDelete: [UUID]; let decisionsToInsert: [DecisionLogEntry]; let metadata: AppMetadata }
interfaces.notes: Transactional write payload emitted by RitualEngine and applied atomically by ProjectRepository. Agreed unchanged since round 1.

interfaces.name: LooseEndsRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol LooseEndsRepository: Sendable { func loadSnapshot() async throws -> RitualSnapshot; func apply(_ plan: RepositoryMutationPlan) async throws; func replaceMetadata(_ metadata: AppMetadataRecord) async throws; func databaseFileURL() throws -> URL }
interfaces.notes: Single persistence boundary; implementation is SQLite-backed.

interfaces.name: SQLiteDatabase
interfaces.signature: protocol SQLiteDatabase: Sendable { func open(at url: URL) throws; func execute(_ sql: String, bindings: [SQLiteValue]) throws; func transaction<T>(_ body: () throws -> T) throws -> T; func query(_ sql: String, bindings: [SQLiteValue]) throws -> [[String: SQLiteValue]] }
interfaces.notes: Thin internal wrapper over libsqlite3; not exposed to UI.

interfaces.name: NowProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol NowProviding: Sendable { func now() -> Date }
interfaces.notes: Injectable time source for deterministic tests.

interfaces.name: EntitlementProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol EntitlementProviding: Sendable { var hasUnlimitedProjects: Bool { get }; var maxFreeActiveProjects: Int { get } }
interfaces.notes: Sealed boundary. FreeTierEntitlements is the only implementation today, no StoreKit calls. Cap remains intentionally unenforced — a regression test must assert the 8th/9th/10th project still creates successfully.

interfaces.name: EntitlementState
interfaces.signature: enum EntitlementState: Sendable { case free; case paid }
interfaces.notes: Currently always free in MVP.

interfaces.name: LooseEndsStore
interfaces.signature: protocol LooseEndsStore: AnyObject { var homeState: HomeScreenState { get }; var history: [HistoryEntryViewData] { get }; var projects: [ProjectListItemViewData] { get }; var currentError: AppError? { get }; func load() async; func createProject(_ draft: ProjectDraft) async; func editProject(id: ProjectID, draft: ProjectDraft) async; func deleteProject(id: ProjectID) async; func act(on projectID: ProjectID, nextStep: String) async; func snooze(on projectID: ProjectID, interval: SnoozeIntervalOption) async; func release(on projectID: ProjectID, note: String?) async }
interfaces.notes: Main-actor observable app store used by all screens.

interfaces.name: BackupInclusionVerifier
interfaces.signature: func verifyBackupInclusion(for fileURL: URL) throws -> Bool
interfaces.notes: Both sides independently specified this exact test utility (Codex: BackupInclusionVerifier, Claude: BackupExclusionChecker) — same purpose, trivial naming reconciliation only.

interfaces.name: ThreadGlyphAccessibilityLabel
interfaces.signature: static func threadGlyphAccessibilityLabel(_ state: ThreadStatus, now: Date) -> String
interfaces.notes: Single source of truth so the visual glyph tier and its VoiceOver string can never drift apart — both read the same ThreadStatus associated value.

interfaces.name: Project
interfaces.signature: struct Project: Identifiable, Sendable { let id: UUID; var title: String; var whatItIs: String; var whyItMattered: String; var whyItStalled: String; var smallestNextStep: String?; var lastTouchedAt: Date; let createdAt: Date; var updatedAt: Date; var status: ProjectStatus; var snoozeUntil: Date?; var snoozeCount: Int; var releasedAt: Date?; var releaseNote: String? }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED. Surfaced-ness, dormancy, and snooze-eligibility are all derived at read time (metadata.surfacedProjectID + timestamp fields), never stored redundantly on the row. Editing never resets lastTouchedAt or status.

interfaces.name: SnoozeInterval
interfaces.signature: enum SnoozeInterval: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case twoWeeks, oneMonth, threeMonths; var duration: TimeInterval { get } }
interfaces.notes: Only these three fixed intervals, no custom dates.

interfaces.name: DecisionType
interfaces.signature: enum DecisionType: String, Codable, Sendable { case act, snooze, release }
interfaces.notes: Delete never produces a DecisionType/decision row.

interfaces.name: ThreadStatus
interfaces.signature: enum ThreadStatus: Sendable { case frayed(daysUntouched: Int); case taut; case coiled(until: Date); case knotted }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED on the associated-value shape (not tiered-only cases) so the exact day count / snooze date rides into the type for literal VoiceOver labels ('untouched for 47 days', 'snoozed until August 12'). Visual tier-bucketing (light/moderate/heavy fraying) happens inside ThreadGlyph's rendering, off the same integer the label reads — cannot drift apart. History rows only ever resolve to taut/coiled/knotted.

interfaces.name: DecisionLogEntry
interfaces.signature: struct DecisionLogEntry: Identifiable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let projectID: UUID; let projectTitleAtDecision: String; let type: DecisionType; let occurredAt: Date; var note: String?; var resultingSnoozeInterval: SnoozeInterval?; var resultingNextEligibleAt: Date? }
interfaces.notes: Append-only, immutable. Denormalized snapshot fields (title, resulting next-eligible date) frozen at write time so later project corrections never rewrite history. Both sides converged on this independently in round 1.

interfaces.name: StoreSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct StoreSnapshot: Sendable { var projects: [Project]; var decisions: [DecisionLogEntry]; var metadata: AppMetadata }
interfaces.notes: Whole-app in-memory snapshot; the only thing loaded from and written back to the repository.

interfaces.name: HomeState
interfaces.signature: enum HomeState: Sendable { case loading; case surfaced(SurfacedProjectViewData); case noProjectsYet; case noneEligibleYet(reason: String, nextEligibleAt: Date?); case allReleased; case storeFailure(AppError) }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED. Computed entirely inside RitualEngine.bootstrap; Home view never reinterprets raw models or derives day-counts/glyphs itself.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.eligibleProjects
interfaces.signature: static func eligibleProjects(in projects: [Project], now: Date) -> [Project]
interfaces.notes: 14-day dormancy boundary + snoozeUntil gate + status != .released; negative elapsed always resolves not-eligible.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.selectSurfaced
interfaces.signature: static func selectSurfaced(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) -> UUID?
interfaces.notes: Only runs when metadata.surfacedProjectID == nil and (lastResolvedAt == nil or elapsed >= 7 days). Tiebreak: oldest lastTouchedAt -> oldest createdAt -> UUID string.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.homeState
interfaces.signature: static func homeState(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) -> HomeState
interfaces.notes: Pure derivation the UI never reimplements.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.resolveAct
interfaces.signature: static func resolveAct(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, projectID: UUID, nextStep: String, now: Date) throws -> (StoreSnapshot, DecisionLogEntry)
interfaces.notes: Resets lastTouchedAt to now, status back to .active, clears surfacedProjectID, sets lastResolvedAt = now.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.resolveSnooze
interfaces.signature: static func resolveSnooze(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, projectID: UUID, interval: SnoozeInterval, now: Date) throws -> (StoreSnapshot, DecisionLogEntry)
interfaces.notes: Does not touch lastTouchedAt. Sets snoozeUntil = now + interval.duration, increments snoozeCount, clears surfacedProjectID, sets lastResolvedAt = now.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.resolveRelease
interfaces.signature: static func resolveRelease(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, projectID: UUID, closureNote: String?, now: Date) throws -> (StoreSnapshot, DecisionLogEntry)
interfaces.notes: status -> .released (terminal), clears surfacedProjectID, sets lastResolvedAt = now. Never color/negatively framed downstream.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.deleteProject
interfaces.signature: static func deleteProject(id: UUID, snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) -> RepositoryMutationPlan
interfaces.notes: No decision row produced. If id was the surfaced project, clears the slot and lets the next bootstrap/selectSurfaced call re-fill it without touching lastResolvedAt — deletion never consumes the week's pacing lock.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.validateLastTouchedDate
interfaces.signature: static func validateLastTouchedDate(_ input: Date, now: Date) -> Date
interfaces.notes: Clamps any future-dated input to now at entry time.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.recoverSurfacedState
interfaces.signature: static func recoverSurfacedState(snapshot: StoreSnapshot) -> (StoreSnapshot, recovered: Bool)
interfaces.notes: Re-derives the correct surfaced pointer from the decision log if metadata is inconsistent, or falls back to nil. Feeds RitualComputation.recoveredFromCorruption and AppStore.didRecoverFromCorruptionOnLastLoad.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.threadStatus(for:now:)
interfaces.signature: static func threadStatus(for project: Project, now: Date) -> ThreadStatus
interfaces.notes: Used for the live Home card; only path that can ever produce .frayed.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.threadStatus(for decision:)
interfaces.signature: static func threadStatus(for type: DecisionType) -> ThreadStatus
interfaces.notes: act -> .taut, snooze -> .coiled, release -> .knotted. Used exclusively by History rows.

interfaces.name: AppStoreError
interfaces.signature: enum AppStoreError: Error, Sendable { case loadFailed; case saveFailed; case notFound; case validationFailed(field: String, reason: String); case recoveredCorruption(reason: String) }
interfaces.notes: Typed error surface for UI error states. No silent failure or optimistic UI without persistence success.

interfaces.name: ClockProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol ClockProviding: Sendable { func now() -> Date }
interfaces.notes: SystemClock is the production implementation. AppStore and ProjectRepository both source time only from clock.now() — nothing past the UI ever calls Date() directly, enabling end-to-end time-travel tests.

interfaces.name: ProjectRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol ProjectRepository { func loadSnapshot() throws -> StoreSnapshot; func apply(_ plan: RepositoryMutationPlan) throws; func databaseFileURL() throws -> URL }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED: fully synchronous, no actor, called directly from the main-actor AppStore. A required perf test asserts a full mutation transaction against a database pre-loaded with several hundred history rows completes under 10ms; failing that test is the actual trigger to revisit this, not aesthetic preference.

interfaces.name: BackupExclusionChecker
interfaces.signature: static func isExcludedFromBackup(fileURL: URL) throws -> Bool
interfaces.notes: Reads URLResourceValues.isExcludedFromBackup on the live SQLite file; automated test asserts this is always false, independent of storage technology.

interfaces.name: RedactedLogger
interfaces.signature: static func log(_ event: String, projectID: UUID?)
interfaces.notes: Never accepts or interpolates user free-text content (titles, notes) — static event names and opaque IDs only.

interfaces.name: AppStore
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class AppStore { private(set) var homeState: HomeState; private(set) var history: [HistoryEntryViewData]; private(set) var projects: [ProjectListItemViewData]; private(set) var lastError: AppError?; private(set) var didRecoverFromCorruptionOnLastLoad: Bool; init(repository: ProjectRepository, clock: ClockProviding = SystemClock()); func bootstrap(); func createProject(_ draft: ProjectDraft); func updateProject(_ project: Project); func act(on projectID: UUID, nextStep: String); func snooze(on projectID: UUID, interval: SnoozeInterval); func release(on projectID: UUID, closureNote: String?); func deleteProject(id: UUID) }
interfaces.notes: RESOLVED: every method synchronous, called directly from SwiftUI button/form actions with no Task{} wrapping anywhere — avoids the double-tap race a Task-wrapped call would allow. Kind listed as struct/protocol for schema purposes; actual declaration is @MainActor @Observable final class.

interfaces.name: ThreadGlyph
interfaces.signature: struct ThreadGlyph: View { let status: ThreadStatus; var body: some View { get }; var accessibilityLabel: String { get } }
interfaces.notes: Visual tier-bucketing for .frayed happens inside this view's rendering, off the same daysUntouched integer the accessibility label reads.

interfaces.name: DateChip
interfaces.signature: struct DateChip: View { enum Kind { case untouchedFor(days: Int); case nextEligible(Date); case snoozePreview(SnoozeInterval, resultingDate: Date) }; let kind: Kind }
interfaces.notes: One shared component for every date-preview usage across Home, none-eligible state, and the Snooze sheet confirmation.

interfaces.name: AppMetadata
interfaces.signature: struct AppMetadata: Codable, Sendable { var schemaVersion: Int; var surfacedProjectID: UUID?; var lastResolvedAt: Date?; var hasCompletedOnboarding: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Single-row metadata table. Surfaced slot and pacing timestamp are explicit persisted state, never query-derived. hasCompletedOnboarding lives here (not UserDefaults) so wiping/resetting the store correctly resets onboarding too.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.bootstrap
interfaces.signature: static func bootstrap(snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) -> RitualComputation
interfaces.notes: Composes recoverSurfacedState + selectSurfaced (if slot empty and pacing allows) + view-data assembly into the single call AppStore.bootstrap() needs.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.createProject
interfaces.signature: static func createProject(draft: ProjectDraft, snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan
interfaces.notes: Applies validateLastTouchedDate. Never blocked by the free-tier cap — that gate is a UI/EntitlementProviding concern, not a domain rule.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.updateProject
interfaces.signature: static func updateProject(_ project: Project, snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan
interfaces.notes: Never resets lastTouchedAt or status, under any circumstance, including direct snooze-until correction from the edit screen.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.act
interfaces.signature: static func act(on projectID: UUID, nextStep: String, snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan
interfaces.notes: Resets lastTouchedAt = now, clears metadata.surfacedProjectID, sets lastResolvedAt = now.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.snooze
interfaces.signature: static func snooze(on projectID: UUID, interval: SnoozeInterval, snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan
interfaces.notes: Never touches lastTouchedAt. Sets snoozeUntil = now + interval.duration, increments snoozeCount, clears surfacedProjectID, sets lastResolvedAt = now.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.release
interfaces.signature: static func release(on projectID: UUID, closureNote: String?, snapshot: StoreSnapshot, now: Date) throws -> RepositoryMutationPlan
interfaces.notes: status -> .released, sets releasedAt/releaseNote, clears surfacedProjectID, sets lastResolvedAt = now. Never color-coded as failure downstream.

interfaces.name: RitualEngine.threadStatus(for:)
interfaces.signature: static func threadStatus(for decision: DecisionLogEntry) -> ThreadStatus
interfaces.notes: Used exclusively by History rows; maps act/snooze/release to taut/coiled(until:)/knotted.
